audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for prologue, Zechariah/Elizabeth, Gabriel, John announcement, Mary announcement, Mary/Elizabeth, Magnificat, John birth, Benedictus, Spirit/prophecy/mercy/covenant language.

verses.restored: Since many have undertaken to arrange an account concerning the matters fulfilled among us,
just as those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed them down to us,
it seemed good to me also, having followed all things closely from the first, to write them in order for you, most excellent Theophilus,
so that you may know the secure certainty concerning the words in which you were instructed.
In the days of Herod, king of Judea, there was a certain priest named Zechariah, from the division of Abijah, and his wife from the daughters of Aaron; her name was Elizabeth.
They were both righteous before the Divine, walking blamelessly in all the commands and ordinances of the Lord.
They had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and both were advanced in their days.
It happened while he was serving as priest before the Divine in the order of his division,
according to the custom of the priesthood, he was chosen by lot to enter the sanctuary of the Lord and burn incense.
The whole multitude of the people was praying outside at the hour of incense.
A messenger of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.
Zechariah was troubled when he saw, and fear fell upon him.
But the messenger said to him, Do not fear, Zechariah, because your prayer has been heard; your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will call his name John.
There will be joy and gladness for you, and many will rejoice at his birth.
For he will be great before the Lord; wine and strong drink he will certainly not drink, and he will be filled with holy Spirit even from his mother's womb.
He will turn many of the children of Israel back to the Lord their Divine.
He will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn hearts of fathers toward children and disobedient ones into the wisdom of the righteous, to prepare for the Lord a people made ready.
Zechariah said to the messenger, By what shall I know this? For I am old, and my wife is advanced in her days.
The messenger answered, I am Gabriel, the one standing before the Divine; I was sent to speak to you and announce these good things to you.
Look, you will be silent and unable to speak until the day these things happen, because you did not trust my words, which will be fulfilled in their time.
The people were waiting for Zechariah and marveling at his delay in the sanctuary.
Coming out, he was not able to speak to them; they recognized that he had seen a vision in the sanctuary. He was making signs to them and remained mute.
When the days of his service were completed, he went to his house.
After these days Elizabeth his wife conceived, and she kept herself hidden five months, saying,
Thus the Lord has done for me in the days when he looked upon me, to take away my reproach among people.
In the sixth month, the messenger Gabriel was sent from the Divine to a city of Galilee named Nazareth,
to a virgin betrothed to a man named Joseph, from the house of David; the virgin's name was Mary.
Coming to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one; the Lord is with you.
She was deeply disturbed at the word and was reasoning what kind of greeting this might be.
The messenger said to her, Do not fear, Mary, for you have found favor with the Divine.
Look, you will conceive in your womb and bear a son, and you will call his name Jesus.
He will be great and will be called Son of the Most High; the Lord Divine will give him the throne of David his father.
He will reign over the house of Jacob into the ages, and of his reign there will be no end.
Mary said to the messenger, How will this be, since I do not know a man?
The messenger answered her, Holy Spirit will come upon you, and power of the Most High will overshadow you; therefore the holy one being born will be called Son of the Divine.
Look, Elizabeth your relative has also conceived a son in her old age; this is the sixth month for her who was called barren.
For no word from the Divine will be impossible.
Mary said, Look, the servant of the Lord; let it happen to me according to your word. And the messenger departed from her.
In those days Mary rose and went with haste into the hill country, to a city of Judah.
She entered the house of Zechariah and greeted Elizabeth.
When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby leapt in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with holy Spirit.
She cried out with a great voice and said, Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb.
How has this happened to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
For look, when the voice of your greeting came into my ears, the baby in my womb leapt in gladness.
Blessed/flourishing is she who trusted that there will be fulfillment of the things spoken to her from the Lord.
Mary said, My soul magnifies the Lord,
and my spirit rejoices in the Divine my Savior,
because he looked upon the lowliness of his servant. For look, from now all generations will call me blessed,
because the Mighty One has done great things for me, and holy is his name.
His mercy is into generations and generations for those who reverence him.
He has shown strength with his arm; he has scattered the proud in the reasoning of their hearts.
He has pulled down rulers from thrones and lifted up the lowly.
The hungry he has filled with good things, and the rich he has sent away empty.
He has helped Israel his servant, remembering mercy,
just as he spoke to our fathers, to Abraham and to his seed into the age.
Mary remained with her about three months and returned to her house.
For Elizabeth, the time was fulfilled for her to give birth, and she bore a son.
Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord magnified his mercy with her, and they rejoiced with her.
On the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they were calling him by his father's name, Zechariah.
His mother answered, No; he will be called John.
They said to her, There is no one from your relatives who is called by this name.
They were signaling to his father what he might wish him to be called.
Asking for a writing tablet, he wrote, His name is John. They all marveled.
Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue released, and he spoke, blessing the Divine.
Fear came upon all those living around them, and all these words were being spoken throughout the whole hill country of Judea.
All who heard placed them in their hearts, saying, What then will this child be? For the hand of the Lord was with him.
Zechariah his father was filled with holy Spirit and prophesied, saying,
Blessed is the Lord, the Divine of Israel, because he has visited and made redemption for his people.
He has raised a horn of salvation for us in the house of David his servant,
just as he spoke through the mouth of his holy prophets from of old,
salvation from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us,
to do mercy with our fathers and to remember his holy covenant,
the oath that he swore to Abraham our father, to grant us
that, rescued from the hand of enemies, we might serve him without fear,
in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.
And you, child, will be called prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his ways,
to give knowledge of salvation to his people in release of their sins/misalignments,
through the deep mercy of our Divine, by which the dawn from on high will visit us,
to shine upon those sitting in darkness and death's shadow, to guide our feet into the way of peace.
The child grew and became strong in spirit, and he was in the wilderness until the day of his public appearance to Israel.
